items.title: Inside Wisconsin’s Wild Spearing Tradition for Prehistoric Fish
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Joe Barrett reports from Lake Winnebago to explain why locals take to the ice in pursuit of a creature that looks a lot like a prehistoric catfish.

#Wisconsin #Fishing #WSJ</article>

items.title: The K-Shaped Economy Is Testing Stocks Like Walmart and General Mills
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Spending differences between high and low-income households are testing seemingly safe consumer staple stocks. WSJ’s Aaron Back explains the movement in the sector.

#Economy #Stocks #WSJ</article>

items.title: Watch: Trump's Full Response to Supreme Court’s Tariff Decision | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>The Supreme Court ruled that President Trump's global tariffs are illegal. It is the first time the high court has definitively struck down one of Trump’s second-term policies.

#Trump #Tariffs #WSJ</article>

items.title: Supreme Court Strikes Down Trump’s Tariffs: What’s Next?
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>The Supreme Court struck down President Trump’s global tariffs, removing a key tool Trump used to remake U.S. trade deals. WSJ’s Ashby Jones explains the implications of the court’s decision.

#SCOTUS #Tariffs #WSJ</article>

items.title: Why Everyone Wants to Crack Norway's Secret for Olympic Domination
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>How does a country with the population of Minnesota dominate the entire world in the Winter Olympics? WSJ's Ben Cohen explains how Norway has pushed its complete and utter ownership of this event to the extreme. ⁠

#Norway #Olympics #WSJ</article>

items.title: Former Prince Andrew Arrested Amid Epstein Links: What We Know
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>For the first time in modern history, a member of the British royal family has been taken into policy custody. WSJ’s Max Colchester explains why Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor was arrested now.

#Britain #Epstein #WSJ</article>

items.title: What Is Skimo? The New Olympic Sport, Explained in 60 Seconds
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Ski mountaineering, or "skimo," involves running up a mountain and then tearing down the slopes. WSJ's Laine Higgins explains how it works.⁠

#Skiing #Olympics #WSJ</article>

items.title: What It Takes to Build a Modern Nuclear Shelter for 7K People | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>As Russia’s war with Ukraine threatens to spill over into Europe, nations around the world are rapidly restoring and expanding their network of shelters to keep people safe in the event of a chemical, biological or even nuclear attack. Shelter construction companies have been heading to Finland and Switzerland to see what it takes to build a bunker that can withstand modern day threats.

WSJ traveled to Finland to check out one of the country’s largest modern nuclear shelters that can keep 7,000 residents in the Savilhati region safe. 

Chapters:
0:00 Inside the Finnish bunker
1:15 The outer perimeter and blast doors
2:52 The lungs and the beds 
5:19 Why manufacturers are headed to Finland

#Bunkers #Finland #WSJ</article>

items.title: Can Team USA Return to the Olympic Podium in Women's Figure Skating?
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>An American woman hasn't medaled in individual figure skating in 20 years. WSJ’s Louise Radnofsky looks at the chances for the podium among the favorites. ⁠

#Olympics #Skating #WSJ</article>

items.title: Billionaire Ken Ricci Explains Why Your Network Is So Important
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>If you lost every dollar, but kept your network, how long until you’re rich again? Here’s what billionaire Kenn Ricci says.⁠
⁠
WSJ’s Gunjan Banerji sat down with the Flexjet chairman to discuss the secrets of his success and the mistakes he learned along the way.⁠

#Business #Wealth #WSJ</article>

items.title: Mikaela Shiffrin Skis to Olympic Redemption with Slalom Gold
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Mikaela Shiffrin ended her medal drought with a victory in her best event. She now has more golds than any other U.S. alpine skier in the history of the Games. WSJ's Rachel Bachman reports from Cortina.

#Skiing #Olympics #WSJ</article>

items.title: The Real Competition at the Milan Games? Olympics vs. Opera
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>The Olympics have invaded Milan—but Italians would rather watch a 5-hour opera.⁠
⁠
During the Games, La Scala, the world’s most storied opera house, is producing only one opera, an epic tale about a world consumed by cleansing fire.⁠

#Olympics #Italy #WSJ</article>

items.title: Why Robinhood Wants Trading to Feel Like a Sport
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Robinhood’s CEO Vlad Tenev is betting that if you make investing feel more entertaining, more people will participate. But critics say the platform feels more like a casino than a brokerage.

#Robinhood #Business #WSJ</article>

items.title: The Hidden Government Funding of China's U.S.-Born Olympic Star
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>After details of payments from Beijing’s sports bureau to freestyle skiing champion Eileen Gu were accidentally revealed last year, her name was quickly scrubbed from the budget.⁠

#Olympics #China #WSJ</article>
